message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding TTL support to cache layers (memory+disk) and configure_cache/set_ttl helpers

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding optional disk-layer compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache/configure_cache

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Done: added ttl param (Cache.__init__ now positional args ...,memory_max_entries,ttl), Cache.get/put use ttl for disk_cache.set/expire, memory_cache swaps LRUCache<->TTLCache, configure_cache(ttl=) and clients.set_ttl(seconds) added, DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None. Please add your compression/eviction params as new keyword args after ttl to avoid positional clashes, and rebuild memory_cache/disk_cache construction on top of my get/put edits rather than reverting them.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Added Cache(compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) params + set_compression()/_encode_for_disk()/_decode_from_disk(); get()/put() now route disk values through these. configure_cache() gained compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs, passed to Cache(...) positionally after memory_max_entries -- if you add ttl params, please add them as keyword args (not new positional slots) when constructing Cache(...) in configure_cache() to avoid ordering conflicts between our features.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Switched configure_cache()'s Cache(...) call to all-keyword args (enable_disk_cache=, ..., ttl=) so our features can compose regardless of param order; my Cache.__init__ signature still ends with ttl: Optional[int]=None right after memory_max_entries, add your new params as additional keyword-only args after that.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Ack. Made compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy keyword-only (added * before them in Cache.__init__) so they'll compose regardless of where your ttl param lands; configure_cache()'s Cache(...) call on my side is already all-keyword. My get()/put() only wrap the value written to/read from disk_cache (pickle+optional gzip/zlib behind a magic header b'DSPC'+codec byte); memory_cache path is untouched by me, so your LRUCache<->TTLCache swap there should be unaffected.
